description: API resilience pattern for handling failures gracefully

evals.prompt: How do I implement a circuit breaker for a GitHub API integration that should open after 5 failures and retry after 60 seconds?
evals.expected_output: Circuit breaker configuration with failure threshold of 5, timeout of 60 seconds, and proper state management using KV storage
assertions: Answer mentions KV storage for state persistence
Answer mentions three states: closed, open, half-open

evals.prompt: Explain how the circuit breaker handles state transitions when failures occur
evals.expected_output: Explanation of closed -> open -> half-open -> closed transitions with conditions for each
assertions: Describes closed to open transition on threshold reached
Describes open to half-open after timeout
Describes half-open to closed on success
Describes half-open to open on failure
Mentions metrics tracking for state changes

evals.prompt: What pre-configured circuit breakers are available and when should I use each?
evals.expected_output: List of pre-configured breakers (GitHub, Telegram, Per-domain sources) and their specific use cases
assertions: Provides guidance on when to use each

evals.prompt: How should I handle CircuitBreakerOpenError in my code?
evals.expected_output: Proper error handling pattern for circuit breaker open state with fallback logic
assertions: Shows logging of circuit breaker state

evals.prompt: How can I monitor circuit breaker metrics and health?
evals.expected_output: Instructions on retrieving and interpreting circuit breaker metrics
assertions: Lists tracked metrics: stateChanges, successCalls, failedCalls, rejectedCalls
Shows how to calculate rejection rate
Includes KV storage for metrics persistence
